_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **ACTION REQUIRED — Register or confirm registration status with the DfT SAF Mandate Administrator.** Any entity in the workspace's supply chain that owns and supplies 15.9 TJ or more (~468,000 litres) of relevant aviation turbine fuel for use in the UK in an obligation year (1 January–31 December) is legally obligated to register. Account opening must begin within 28 days of first becoming an obligated supplier. Parties already registered under the RTFO do not need to re-register but must notify the Administrator of their SAF Mandate obligation. Failure to register carries civil penalty exposure. Immediate action: confirm whether any fuel-supply counterparty in the workspace's UK air-freight operations meets or approaches the threshold. *Source: Renewable Transport Fuel Obligations (SAF) Order 2024, Art. 6; SAF Mandate Compliance Guidance 2025, DfT, Jan 2025. https://assets.publishing.service.gov.uk/media/6762717b26a2d1ff182534f7/saf-mandate-compliance-guidance-2025.pdf*

- **COST ALERT — Quantify buy-out exposure in UK-routed air-freight contracts.** Fuel suppliers unable to meet their SAF Mandate obligation must pay a buy-out price of £4.70 per litre for the main SAF obligation and £5.00 per litre for the PtL obligation. These costs will propagate into aviation fuel surcharges affecting all UK air-freight lanes (Americas–UK, Europe–UK, Asia–UK). Deadline: 15 September of the year immediately following each obligation period is the certificate redemption deadline; buy-out invoicing follows shortly after. Review carrier and fuel-supplier contractual pass-through clauses now before the 2025 obligation period closes (31 December 2025). *Source: SAF Mandate: An Essential Guide, DfT, Dec 2024. https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/about-the-saf-mandate/the-saf-mandate-an-essential-guide; GreenAir News, Jul 2024. https://www.greenairnews.com/?p=5627*

- **MONITORING — Track the SAF Bill (Revenue Certainty Mechanism) as it progresses through Parliament.** The SAF Bill introduced on 14 May 2025 has reached Report Stage in the House of Commons and is expected to become law by end of 2026. The resulting Revenue Certainty Mechanism (a Guaranteed Strike Price / contract-for-difference model) will materially affect long-term SAF pricing and may alter future fuel-surcharge trajectories on UK air-freight lanes critical to the workspace's live events, fine art, luxury, film/TV, and high-value automotive verticals. Assign a regulatory-monitoring owner and set a 90-day review cadence. *Source: Addleshaw Goddard LLP, May 2025. https://www.addleshawgoddard.com/en/insights/insights-briefings/2025/energy/uks-sustainable-aviation-fuel-bill-revenue-certainty-mechanism/*

- **COMPETITIVE EDGE — Engage UK carrier and fuel-supplier partners on SAF certificate strategy.** SAF certificates are tradeable on the open market. Carriers and fuel suppliers that over-supply SAF earn surplus certificates they can sell. Proactively negotiating SAF certificate or blending commitments into carrier agreements for the workspace's high-frequency UK air lanes (e.g. Heathrow, Stansted, Manchester) could lock in cost certainty and support clients' Scope 3 / sustainability reporting requirements, particularly for live events and film/TV productions with public ESG visibility. No fixed deadline, but certificate market pricing is live as of December 2025. *Source: Argus Media, Dec 2025. https://www.argusmedia.com/en/about-argus/media-centre/press-releases/uk-sustainable-aviation-fuel-certificate-prices*

- **WINDOW CLOSING — Capture 2025 compliance-year data before obligation period ends 31 December 2025.** Suppliers may submit data up to 7 months after the obligation period ends; however, final figures are published the following winter. The workspace should ensure UK fuel-volume and sustainability data are collected from carrier and fuel-supplier partners before year-end to support any internal carbon accounting, client sustainability reporting, or regulatory queries. *Source: SAF Mandate Statistics, DfT, Feb 2026. https://www.gov.uk/government/collections/sustainable-aviation-fuel-saf-mandate-statistics*
